title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: NetworkManager bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for NetworkManager is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: NetworkManager is a system network service that manages network devices and connections, attempting to keep active network connectivity when available. Its capabilities include managing Ethernet, wireless, mobile broadband (WWAN), and PPPoE devices, as well as providing VPN integration with a variety of different VPN services.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): Lose VLAN filtering after reapply linux bridge port [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-102742)  NM deletes ipv6 static routes for dualstack Iface after connection up for other ipv4 only Iface  [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-102771)  Can not change `bridge.options.mcast-snooping-enable` on partial managemd OVS bridge [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-87167)  Devices not becoming managed sometimes [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-93183)  [NMCI] ovs_datapath_type_netdev_with_cloned_mac is racey [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-98550)

full_text:
An update for NetworkManager is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

NetworkManager is a system network service that manages network devices and connections, attempting to keep active network connectivity when available. Its capabilities include managing Ethernet, wireless, mobile broadband (WWAN), and PPPoE devices, as well as providing VPN integration with a variety of different VPN services.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): Lose VLAN filtering after reapply linux bridge port [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-102742)  NM deletes ipv6 static routes for dualstack Iface after connection up for other ipv4 only Iface  [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-102771)  Can not change `bridge.options.mcast-snooping-enable` on partial managemd OVS bridge [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-87167)  Devices not becoming managed sometimes [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-93183)  [NMCI] ovs_datapath_type_netdev_with_cloned_mac is racey [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-98550)
